title: feat: add --json output mode to process_downloads.py
goal: The script can output a structured JSON summary to stdout when invoked with --json.
context:
Script: scripts/process_downloads.py
Brief: docs/briefs/process-downloads-agent.md
The script currently logs to text log and audit JSONL. We need a machine-readable output mode for the agent layer.
steps: When `--json` is passed, collect all processing results into a list of dicts with keys: filename, action, destination, status, error (nullable).
At the end of the run, print the JSON array to stdout (one line, compact via `json.dumps`).
Ensure the existing text log and audit JSONL outputs are unaffected when `--json` is not passed.
Test: run `python3 scripts/process_downloads.py --json --dry-run` and verify output is valid JSON.
acceptance_criteria: Running `python3 scripts/process_downloads.py --json --dry-run` produces valid JSON on stdout
Existing log output is unchanged when --json is not passed
JSON output includes all processed items with their status
No secrets or absolute paths leak into the JSON output

title: feat: create rules file loader for tv_name_map and skip_patterns
goal: The script loads name corrections and skip patterns from a JSON config file before processing.
context:
Script: scripts/process_downloads.py
New file: config/process-downloads-rules.json
Brief: docs/briefs/process-downloads-agent.md
The rules file will be maintained by the agent (learning loop) and version-controlled.
steps: In `process_downloads.py`, add a function `load_rules(path)` that reads the JSON file. Default path: `config/process-downloads-rules.json` relative to workspace root.
Apply `tv_name_map` lookups after the initial title parse — if the parsed show name matches a key, replace it with the value.
Apply `skip_patterns` — if any pattern appears in the filename, skip processing that item entirely.
Add a `--rules-file` CLI flag to override the default path.
Test: create a rules file with a known mapping, process a matching filename in dry-run mode, verify the corrected name is used.
acceptance_criteria: config/process-downloads-rules.json exists and is valid JSON
Script applies tv_name_map corrections during processing
Script works normally if rules file is missing (graceful fallback)
